phrases.intent_en: Ask which platform my TGV departs from
phrases.text_local: De quelle voie part mon TGV, s'il vous plaît ?
phrases.pronunciation: duh kel VWAH par mohn tay-zhay-VAY, seel voo PLEH
phrases.cultural_note: TGV platforms at Gare de Lyon are often not announced until 10-15 minutes before departure — a common surprise for first-time travelers. Wait in the main hall and watch the departure boards (panneaux). For TGVs, you must validate (composter) your ticket before boarding.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where to validate (stamp) the train ticket
phrases.text_local: Où se trouvent les composteurs pour valider mon billet ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo suh TROOV lay kohm-poh-TUR poor vah-lee-DAY mohn bee-YEH
phrases.cultural_note: In France, you must 'composter' (validate/stamp) your paper train ticket in the yellow machines near the platforms before boarding. Failure to do so can result in a fine from the inspector, even if you bought a valid ticket. E-tickets on your phone do not need composting.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where the luggage storage lockers are
phrases.text_local: Où se trouvent les consignes à bagages ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo suh TROOV lay kohn-SEEN-yuh ah bah-GAHZH
phrases.cultural_note: Luggage storage (consigne) is available at Gare de Lyon — both automated lockers and a staffed left-luggage office. The service is useful if you want to explore Paris before catching an evening TGV. Location is in the main hall near the side entrances.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about the first train to Marseille tomorrow morning
phrases.text_local: À quelle heure part le premier train pour Marseille demain matin ?
phrases.pronunciation: ah kel UR par luh pruh-MYAY trehn poor mar-SAY duh-MEHN mah-TEHN
phrases.cultural_note: Gare de Lyon serves destinations in southeast France (Lyon, Marseille, Nice, Cannes, Avignon), as well as Switzerland (Geneva, Lausanne), and Italy (Turin, Milan). TGV trains to Lyon take around 2 hours; to Marseille about 3 hours.

phrases.intent_en: Ask a fellow passenger if this is the right platform
phrases.text_local: Excusez-moi, c'est bien le quai pour le train de Lyon ?
phrases.pronunciation: ex-koo-ZAY mwah, seh byehn luh KEH poor luh trehn duh lee-OHN
phrases.cultural_note: French passengers are generally helpful when asked for directions. At large stations, other travelers are often a faster source of information than finding an official. The destination on the front of the train and on platform signs will confirm the route.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where the Le Train Bleu restaurant is
phrases.text_local: Où se trouve le restaurant Le Train Bleu dans la gare ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo suh TROOV luh reh-stoh-RAHN luh trehn BLUH dahn lah GAR
phrases.cultural_note: Le Train Bleu restaurant is on the first floor of Gare de Lyon, accessed by the grand staircase from the main hall. It's a classified historic monument with magnificent Belle Époque décor and has operated since 1901. Even a coffee at the bar is a worthwhile experience.
